predictions.description: The Greet function body ran once per successful call, resolving {fn-var:} inputs per call: call 1 literal+whole-token bindings ('Hello, Ada!'), call 2 all defaults ('Hi, world!'), call 3 mixed-text binding resolved in the caller's scope ('Hey, Dr. Ada!'). Call 4 (bad guid) appended nothing.
A whole-token listOfTexts binding hands the function a REAL list (count 2 for the seeded list), while an unbound or empty-string binding falls back to the declared default [] (count 0).
Inputs-only contract: the function returned data by writing to a bot-scoped variable; the last successful call's greeting ('Hey') overwrote the NEVER-SET sentinel.
Binding caller variables into a function does not mutate them: person and seedList retain their initial values after all calls.
